articles.title: Scorching heat: Cuttack & Ganjam order 3-day closure of schools & colleges - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
In view of the prevailing heatwave conditions, the Cuttack district administration on Monday ordered the closure of all government, aided, and private schools from April 21 to April 23, 2026.
Anganwadi centres will also remain closed during this period. However, teachers are required to attend schools and conduct online classes to ensure completion of the syllabus. Colleges across the district will also remain shut, though scheduled examinations will continue as planned.
Additionally, Anganwadi centres have been directed to distribute take-home rations instead of cooked meals. The administration has warned that strict action will be taken against any violation of the order.
3-Day School Closure in Ganjam Amid Heatwave
In view of prevailing conditions, the district administration has announced the closure of all schools for three days from April 21 to April 23.
The order applies to students from kindergarten to Class 12 across the district. However, the ongoing supplementary examinations for Class 5 and Class 8 will continue as per schedule.
Heatwave Advisory in Kendrapara
In view of the rising heat, the district administration has revised school timings. All educational institutions, from Anganwadi centres to Class 12, will now function from 6:30 AM to 9:00 AM until further notice.
Heatwave Continues: 14 Cities Cross 40°C Today
Meanwhile, the heatwave continues to grip Odisha, with at least 14 cities recording temperatures above 40°C today. Titlagarh recorded the highest at 42.5°C, followed by Jharsuguda at 42.4°C and Talcher at 42°C.
Balangir reported 41.8°C, Anugul 41.7°C, and Bhabanipatna 41°C. The soaring temperatures have raised concerns across the state, with authorities urging people to take necessary precautions.
